Example: Logistic/Gompertz Exploratory Plots (lg.*)

Source: vignettes/lg-exploratory-example.qmd 
library(TemporalHazard)
library(hvtiPlotR)

These examples correspond to the lg.* SAS files: exploratory logistic and Gompertz plots used to identify covariate transformations and preliminary functional forms before fitting the parametric hazard model. These are diagnostic/exploratory tools, not the primary analysis.

lg.death.AVC — Logistic/Gompertz plots, AVC death

SAS source: examples/lg.death.AVC.sas

Purpose: For each candidate covariate, fit a logistic (early hazard) or Gompertz (late hazard) model and plot the estimated relationship. This guides covariate transformation decisions (log, power, inverse) before the hazard model.

SAS (original) 

%LOGIT(
  DATA  = AVCS,
  EVENT = DEAD,
  VARS  = AGE STATUS INC_SURG OPMOS MAL COM_IV ORIFICE OP_AGE
);

R translation (runnable M3 example) 

set.seed(5201)
n <- 360
avcs <- data.frame(
  INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.6),
  AGE = rnorm(n, mean = 6, sd = 3),
  STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),
  INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.15),
  OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),
  MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.25),
  COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),
  ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1),
  OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12)
)

lp <- -2.7 + 0.05 * avcs$AGE + 0.20 * avcs$STATUS + 0.55 * avcs$INC_SURG +
  0.03 * avcs$OPMOS + 0.45 * avcs$MAL + 0.60 * avcs$COM_IV +
  0.35 * avcs$ORIFICE + 0.01 * avcs$OP_AGE
avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))

candidates <- c("AGE", "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "OPMOS",
                "MAL", "COM_IV", "ORIFICE", "OP_AGE")

# Fit logistic model for each candidate covariate
logit_fits <- lapply(candidates, function(var) {
  fmla <- as.formula(paste("DEAD ~", var))
  glm(fmla, data = avcs, family = binomial())
})
names(logit_fits) <- candidates

# Inspect: which covariates show monotone relationships?
coef_tables <- lapply(logit_fits, function(f) coef(summary(f)))
coef_tables[["AGE"]]

# Plot univariable covariate effects using hvtiPlotR EDA
hv_obj <- hv_eda(avcs, x = "AGE", y = "DEAD")
plot(hv_obj) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Age at operation (years)",
    y = "Death",
    title = "Exploratory: AGE vs mortality"
  ) +
  hv_theme()

lg.dead30d.AVC — 30-day mortality logistic model

SAS source: examples/lg.dead30d.AVC.sas

Purpose: Logistic regression for early (30-day) mortality after AVC repair. Identifies covariates driving operative risk.

R translation (runnable M3 example) 

# Define 30-day event indicator
avcs$dead30 <- as.integer(avcs$DEAD == 1 & avcs$INT_DEAD <= 1)

fit_30d <- glm(dead30 ~ AGE + STATUS + COM_IV + MAL,
               data = avcs, family = binomial())
summary(fit_30d)

lg.deadlate.AVC — Late mortality logistic model

SAS source: examples/lg.deadlate.AVC.sas

Purpose: Logistic regression restricted to patients surviving 30 days. Identifies covariates driving late (constant-phase) hazard.

R translation (runnable M3 example) 

# Restrict to survivors of operative period
avcs_late <- avcs[avcs$INT_DEAD > 1 | avcs$DEAD == 0, ]
avcs_late$dead_late <- as.integer(avcs_late$DEAD == 1)

fit_late <- glm(dead_late ~ INC_SURG + ORIFICE + STATUS,
                data = avcs_late, family = binomial())
summary(fit_late)

Note on lg.* → hz.* workflow

The typical HAZARD analysis sequence is:

  1. lg.* — identify covariate transformations and functional forms
  2. ac.* — nonparametric estimate for baseline characterization
  3. hz.* — fit parametric hazard function shape
  4. hm.* — stepwise multivariable model
  5. hp.* — predictions from final model
  6. bs.* — bootstrap confidence intervals
  7. hs.* — sensitivity and scenario analyses

On this page
